semanticCore: Strength, might, craft, plotting, punishment, or severe retribution

keyDerivations.meaning: Might, power, punishment, craft, plotting, or severe retribution

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَهُوَ شَدِيدُ الْمِحَالِ (13:13): And He is severe in might/strength (or plotting/retribution against disbelievers).
discardedNoise: Mahall (place/location) - distinct root M-H-L (different letter form).
Mihal (drought years) - common classical meaning but secondary here.
summaryEn: The root refers to absolute might or retribution (Mihal), highlighting God's severe power to punish mockers who dispute His signs.
summaryAr: تشير مادة هذا الجذر إلى القوة والقدرة الشديدة والمكر بالعصاة وعقوبتهم بما يستحقون (محل)، واصفاً بأس الله الشديد بالـ (محال) ضد المعاندين.
rawText: أَمْحَلَتِ النُّجُومُ 4 The stars set aurorally and brought no rain. (S, K * in art. خوى.) تَمَحَّلْتُ مَالًا بِغَيْرِ ثَمَنٍ 5 I laboured to acquire property without price: (Msb:) or, accord. to Az, تَمَحَّلَ مَالًا means he laboured, and exercised art or management, in seeking [to acquire] property. (TA.) See also تَعَلَّثَ. لَبَنٌ مُمَحَّلٌ Sour milk upon which much fresh is milked: see قَارِصٌ. مَحَالَةٌ : see art. حول; and see also فَوْهَآءُ voce أَفْوَهُ, and فَوْقَآءُ voce أَفْوَقُ, and قَبٌّ. مَحْلٌ Drought, or suspension of rain, (S, K, Msb in art. جدب,) and dryness of the earth (S, Msb ubi suprà) depriving it of herbage; (S, TA;) and i. q. جَدْبٌ. (K.) مُتَمَاحِلٌ : see رَدَاحٌ.
